title: Cosmos Remix — 3D Cube Lattice you can retune and reskin live
summary: Remixable particle 3D cube lattice: a regular grid of glowing points filling a cube, rotated around the X and Y axes and perspective-projected so it reads as a rotating, glowing box of points; depth maps to point size and brightness for a real volumetric feel. A live glass control panel retunes palette (brand theme tokens), shape, density, spread, speed and dot size in real time, plus a Layers knob that sets the lattice resolution; reskins to any brand colour in one click. Self-contained, zero dependencies; the ?auto=1 capture hides the panel and renders a deterministic seamless loop.
usage_notes: RUN: open and use the panel (top-right) to retune live — pick a palette to reskin to any brand colour, drag Density/Spread/Speed/Dot size, drag Layers to change the lattice resolution (points per cube edge), press the seed button to cycle palettes, Copy to copy your config. KEY TECHNIQUES: an L×L×L lattice (L = Layers) mapped to the cube [-1,1]³ is built ONCE per Layers value into a point array, then each frame the points are rotated around Y by a full turn and around X at 2/3 rate (so frame(0)===frame(LOOP)) and perspective-projected; depth drives both point size and brightness so near faces glow brighter and read as 3D; particles sample the lattice evenly when the grid is denser than Density; all visuals are a pure function of params + time so the panel updates instantly and the capture loop stays seamless; theme tokens = a PALETTES map driving both a CSS accent var and the per-index colour ramp; window.__params + setParam(key,value) is the contract a generic remix harness uses to auto-build panels for ANY effect. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 hides panel + window.__seek(t). TUNE: add palettes to PALETTES, adjust Layers, extend __params.
